name: The Franklin Boutique Hotel
description: Intimate 7-room boutique hotel on Franklin Street with a black colour palette, salvaged vintage elements, and one of Adelaide's best craft beer venues. Independently operated by Leigh and Luke.

name: Mount Lofty House
description: Heritage boutique hotel built in 1852, independently owned by South Australian hotelier David Horbelt, set on 22 acres of native gardens atop Adelaide's highest summit with Hardy's Verandah Restaurant.

name: Sequoia Lodge
description: Adults-only luxury lodge with 14 suites on 30 acres blending into Adelaide Hills Botanic Gardens, featuring thermal hot pools, eco-certification, and 32 complimentary perks including breakfast.

name: Thorngrove Manor
description: Fiercely independent artistic haven awarded 2 Michelin keys, offering absolute privacy and exclusivity with exceptional personalized service in South Australia's oldest wine-producing region.

name: North Adelaide Heritage Group
description: Proudly South Australian owned collection of 15 unique heritage self-contained luxury properties including manors, cottages, and spa suites in Adelaide's most prestigious precinct, established 1988.

name: Fire Station Inn
description: Luxurious converted historic fire station with three distinctive suites including the famous Fire Engine Suite featuring an actual fire truck, king-size spa baths, and fireman-themed decor.

name: Buxton Manor
description: State Heritage Listed 1908 Arts and Crafts manor house with 4 spacious apartments on half an acre of English gardens, individually decorated with bespoke antiques and polished Baltic floors.

name: Majestic Roof Garden Hotel
description: Flagship 4.5-star property of the South Australian family-owned Majestic Hotels group (founded by John Culshaw in 1986), featuring a rooftop garden terrace in the Adelaide CBD.

name: Majestic Minima Hotel
description: Trendsetting boutique with 46 compact studio art rooms, each uniquely painted by local South Australian artists, featuring king beds and contemporary ensuites in North Adelaide.

name: Majestic Old Lion Apartments
description: 66 fully furnished apartments nestled amongst beautifully landscaped gardens in cosmopolitan North Adelaide, part of the SA family-owned Majestic Hotels group.

name: Majestic Tynte Street Apartments
description: Fully self-contained studio apartments in cosmopolitan North Adelaide with free unlimited Wi-Fi, part of the proudly South Australian family-owned Majestic Hotels group.

name: Majestic M Suites
description: Spacious aparthotel rooms with fully-equipped kitchens and an on-site art gallery on Tynte Street, part of the SA-owned Majestic Hotels collection.

name: NABSA - North Adelaide Boutique Stays Accommodation
description: Modern boutique motel offering 4-star comfort in the heart of historic North Adelaide, minutes from Adelaide Oval and the CBD with wheelchair-friendly facilities.

name: Greenways Apartments
description: Serviced apartments in an iconic 1930s heritage building in North Adelaide, managed by South Australian-owned M&G Hotel Group with free WiFi and parking.

name: Princes Lodge Motel
description: Charming century-old colonial-style motel with classic interiors and arch staircase in the centre of North Adelaide, offering complimentary breakfast.

name: The George Glenelg
description: Glenelg's newest boutique hotel by the SA family-owned Taplin Group (est. 1932), steps from the beach with Soda Rooftop -- Glenelg's first true rooftop destination offering sweeping coastal views.

name: Largs Pier Hotel
description: Heritage-listed beachfront landmark established 1882, offering 38 air-conditioned rooms including sea-view suites with spa baths and a self-contained beachside apartment on the Lefevre Peninsula.

name: Durham Serviced Apartments
description: 56 premium studio apartments blending comfort with cutting-edge design in beachside Glenelg, proudly South Australian owned and operated by Legacy Hotels.

name: Seawall Apartments
description: Award-winning 4-star aparthotel with 19 rooms offering sea views, just 700m from The Beachouse in Glenelg.

name: Marion Hotel
description: Named SA's best hotel in 2020, offering 62 industrial-chic guest rooms with custom king beds, Yoder smoked dishes in the restaurant, and a recently renovated bar and dining complex.

name: The Stirling Hotel
description: Hidden gem in central Stirling with five elegant white-on-white guest rooms above a buzzy bistro and wine bar, featuring crackling fireplaces and freestanding bathtubs.

name: The Crafers Hotel
description: Restored 1839 heritage landmark with seven elegant upstairs rooms featuring plush bedding and modern bathrooms, some with freestanding bathtubs, plus a gastro-pub showcasing local produce.

name: Sticky Rice Villas
description: Three uniquely styled luxury villas (Bali, Yoko, Zen) with 6-star energy rating, on-site Sticky Rice Cooking School, underfloor heating and designer furnishings in Stirling.

name: Bishops Adelaide Hills
description: 300-acre heritage property owned by the Bishop family since 1871, a working cherry orchard offering two restored heritage cottages -- Henry's and The Waterfalls -- 30 minutes from Adelaide.

name: CABN Hahndorf
description: Sustainable off-grid accommodation featuring eight minimalist cabins and one CABN X with in-cabin sauna and outdoor bath, offering a nature escape minutes from historic Hahndorf.

name: Hahndorf House Bed & Breakfast
description: Three fully self-contained private spa cottages with fireplaces, gourmet breakfast provisions, complimentary sparkling and chocolates on arrival, plus Senses Day Spa on site.

name: The Haus Studios
description: Self-contained studio apartments on Hahndorf's Main Street blending contemporary design with homely comfort, suitable for couples, families, and corporate travellers.

name: The Stables Boutique Motel
description: Boutique motel in Hahndorf with free WiFi and parking, known for helpful staff and comfortable rooms in the heart of Australia's oldest surviving German settlement.

name: Amble at Hahndorf
description: Bed and breakfast in Hahndorf's historical district offering free WiFi and parking in a charming heritage setting.

name: Aldgate Valley Bed and Breakfast
description: Self-contained country-styled cottage for couples overlooking Aldgate Creek with gourmet cooked breakfast on a private deck, surrounded by majestic gardens and abundant wildlife.

name: Earthship Adelaide Hills
description: Australia's first council-approved Earthship eco B&B made from recycled car tyres filled with earth, powered by solar panels and batteries with rainwater harvesting.

name: The House on Soul Hill
description: Custom-designed luxury 30sqm cabin for two nestled amongst gums with expansive views across the hills, approximately 2km from Lobethal township.

name: 1965 Lobethal Bed and Breakfast
description: Cosy mid-century themed accommodation with 1960s mod-era decor and contemporary facilities, perfect for one or two couples in the Adelaide Hills.

name: Old Brickworks Lobethal
description: Eclectic and unique accommodation in a converted former mechanic shed alongside heritage brick kiln ruins, offering 2 beds with kitchenette just 2 minutes from Lobethal centre.

name: Jura House
description: Premium homestead sleeping 10 on 65 stunning acres overlooking valleys across Woodside and Lobethal with views to the Ranges, offering intimate luxury for couples or groups.

name: Barristers Block Vigneron Villa
description: Fully renovated house adjacent to Barristers Block Cellar Door and Vineyard in Woodside, surrounded by vineyards, landscaped gardens, and farm life.

name: Camellia Cottage
description: Tranquil one-bedroom retreat in leafy Crafers perched above a historic red gum cottage, blending rustic charm with modern comfort minutes from Stirling Village.

name: Crafers Cottages
description: Self-contained brick cottages including Cherrytree Cottage set in extensive English gardens with mature eucalypts, walking distance to Stirling shopping centre.

name: Studio One Crafers
description: Beautifully appointed fully self-contained holiday accommodation nestled in the heart of one of the oldest towns in the Adelaide Hills.

name: Adelaide Hills House
description: Luxury private retreat in the Adelaide Hills offering a premium accommodation experience as a curated escape.

name: Naiko Retreat
description: Eco-accredited off-grid retreat on a 2000-acre farm perched on cliffs overlooking a private beach, designed by Max Pritchard with solar power, chef-prepared dining, and a rare Futuro pod spa.

name: The Louise
description: Luxury lodge with 15 villa-style suites opening onto private courtyards with vineyard views, birthplace of Luxury Lodges of Australia, home to acclaimed Appellation restaurant.

name: Kingsford The Barossa
description: Award-winning heritage-listed luxury retreat on 225 acres with 13 rooms and suites, a 25-metre wine tunnel, Peppertree Pool & Bar, and Orleana Restaurant.

name: Chateau Tanunda Guesthouses
description: Exclusive heritage guesthouses nestled within the picturesque Chateau Tanunda wine estate, offering an exquisite retreat in the heart of the Barossa.

name: Tanunda House
description: Private exclusive-use 1910 sandstone luxury property on an acre of immaculate gardens with five king bedrooms, swimming pool, and tennis court.

name: Stonewell Cottages & Vineyards
description: Established 1987 as one of the Barossa's first purpose-built vineyard accommodations, offering Haven Spa Suites and cottages overlooking a lake with generous Barossa produce breakfast.

name: Seppeltsfield Vineyard Cottage & Retreat
description: Luxury three-bedroom heritage property first settled in 1852, nestled in Seppeltsfield surrounded by beautiful vineyards and tranquil gardens.

name: The Villas Barossa
description: Two environmentally-sensitive vineyard villas in Marananga on Seppeltsfield Road with CULTIVER linen, soak tubs, and a heritage fruit orchard amid 150-year-old sugar gums.

name: Marananga Cottages
description: Two beautiful bluestone self-contained studio cottages, Kate's and Oscar's, offering continental breakfast and ideal for couples in the heart of Marananga village.

name: Rogasch Cottage
description: Two-bedroom fully-equipped house on a small vineyard with private plunge pool, driving range, micro-winery cellar, and lo-fi charm with experiences from fireside suppers to grape picking.

name: Barossa Guesthouse
description: Two heritage buildings in the heart of Tanunda offering four bedrooms and elegant living spaces for exploring the world-class wine region.

name: Devitt on French B&B

name: Angaston Mews
description: Four self-contained two-bedroom apartments with all amenities including fully equipped kitchens, ideal for families or couples in the historic town of Angaston.

name: Marble Lodge
description: Built of local marble 100m from the street, across gardens from a State and Federal Heritage-Listed homestead in Angaston, ensuring total privacy for B&B guests.

name: Abbotsford Country House
description: Georgian-style farmhouse on 50 acres near Lyndoch with 8 individually decorated suites, wide verandas, and superb country breakfast using local produce.

name: Barossa Country Cottages
description: Two fully self-contained cottages, Geranium and Jacaranda, in an Australian bush garden setting in Lyndoch accommodating up to 10 guests with equipped kitchens.

name: Barossa Pavilions & Glasshouse
description: Six independent self-contained villas alongside the iconic multi-award-winning Barossa Glasshouse as featured on Grand Designs Australia.

name: Barossa Shiraz Estate
description: Six luxurious cottages accommodating two to six guests each in the Southern Barossa, offering vineyard views and wine-country immersion.

name: The Dairyman Barossa
description: Exclusive self-contained on-farm accommodation in two buildings set in the foothills of the Barossa Ranges between Lyndoch and Williamstown.

name: Cambourne Boutique Accommodation
description: Luxury self-contained cottage on 25 acres of premium shiraz vineyard with 3 bedrooms with ensuites and a beautifully appointed kitchen in the Barossa.

name: Benbullen Retreat
description: Five-bedroom bluestone homestead on 10 acres of immaculate parkland, operated by Barossa Grand Retreats, with Hamptons-style kitchen and newly completed pavilion suites.

name: The Vineyard Retreat McLaren Vale
description: Fifteen-acre working vineyard offering six boutique accommodation choices with breathtaking views including the d'Arenberg Cube and Gulf St Vincent, 50 minutes from Adelaide.

name: Oxenberry Farm Cottages
description: Award-winning winery accommodation on the site of McLaren Vale's 1839 first settlers, with luxury self-contained cottages featuring rolling vineyard views, open fireplaces, and claw-footed bathtubs on the Shiraz Trail.

name: Karawatha Cottages

name: Hotel California Road at Inkwell Wines

name: Serafino McLaren Vale

name: Shadow Creek

name: The Old Chaff Mill
description: Restored 19th-century mill in McLaren Vale with two totally private luxury suites, sink-into tubs, modern kitchens, and views of the organic vineyards.

name: Beresford Estate
description: 70-acre vineyard in Blewitt Springs offering 15 modern accommodations from studios to Grand Reserve Suites with private plunge pools, including the remodelled 19th-century Grenache Villa.

name: Divine Domes McLaren Vale

name: Altamira Retreat

name: Rare Earth Retreats

name: McLaren Eye
description: Architectural masterpiece hovering on a hillside in Kangarilla on 50 acres, designed for couples with glass walls providing uninterrupted views and a private helicopter pad.

name: Ivybrook Farm
description: Small family winery and cellar door in rural McLaren Vale offering boutique wine tastings, platters, gourmet pizza, and cottage accommodation.

name: Amanda's Cottage 1899
description: Historic 1899 cottage in complete seclusion in the Kangarilla foothills, welcoming guests since 1995 with fully equipped kitchen, fireplace, and private patio.

name: Bellevue Bed and Breakfast
description: McLaren Vale bed and breakfast offering cooked breakfast, free WiFi, TV, and air conditioning in each room.

name: Vale 194
description: Five Kanmantoo bluestone-fronted holiday homes ranked #1 specialty lodging in McLaren Vale, with polished concrete floors, floating steel staircases, and three bedrooms each.

name: McLaren Vale Motel & Apartments
description: Family-owned and operated since 1981, offering Executive and Deluxe rooms plus studio and 2-bedroom apartments wrapped around by vineyards, 30 minutes from Adelaide CBD.

name: McLaren Vale Studio Apartments
description: Highly rated (9.1 Excellent) studio apartments with kitchen, terrace/patio, and outdoor dining area in the heart of McLaren Vale wine country.

name: The Guest House at Chapel Hill Winery
description: Guest house set within the grounds of one of McLaren Vale's most celebrated wineries, offering vineyard immersion and wine-country lifestyle.

name: Sellicks Chills Vineyard Retreats - The Treehouse
description: Max Pritchard-designed two-storey luxury treehouse in an organic vineyard with unrestricted sea views, king bed, fireplace, and spa-jet bath on deck.

name: Sellicks Chills Vineyard Retreats - The Elephant Door
description: Romantic BOHO space on a working organic vineyard with 4-poster bed, open fireplace, pool table, and round spa-jet bath for two overlooking vineyards and sea.

name: Mulberry Lodge Country Retreat
description: Award-winning hosted luxury retreat in Willunga where owners Hazel and Nik host only one couple or group at a time on 2 acres with gourmet breakfast, complimentary E-Bikes, and vineyard views.

name: McCaffrey Cottage
description: Historic cottage B&B in a beautiful country garden in Willunga, walking distance to the famous Willunga Farmers Market and golf course.

name: The Farm Willunga

name: Ukiyo House

name: Eagle Foundry Bed & Breakfast

name: The Chancellor on Currie
description: 64-room boutique hotel in a near century-old Neo-Georgian building on the corner of Gilbert Place, with heated indoor pool, spa, gym, and personalised service.

name: MacStays
description: Modern quality accommodation in a quiet Woodside location within walking distance of hotels, bakeries, and amenities in the Adelaide Hills.

name: Aldgate Creek Cottage
description: Relaxing cottage retreat in the heart of the Adelaide Hills at Aldgate, perfect for unwinding and exploring the region's wineries and villages.

name: McLaren Vale Getaways
description: Holiday rental accommodation in the McLaren Vale wine region offering self-contained stays surrounded by world-class wineries.

name: Barossa Grand Retreats - Gawler Park Estate
description: Luxury residential-style accommodation in the Barossa Valley alongside their Benbullen property, offering curated wellness and corporate retreat experiences.

name: Queen's Cottage Barossa Valley
description: Heritage cottage accommodation in Williamstown, 39km from Adelaide, featuring air-conditioned rooms and free private parking.

name: Razorback Road Retreat
description: Three self-contained cabins in the Kangarilla countryside with full kitchens accommodating up to four guests each, perfect for slow-paced wine country escapes.

name: Monarto Safari Resort
description: Safari-style accommodation with 78 rooms overlooking the Wild Africa precinct at Monarto Safari Park, about 60 minutes from Adelaide.

name: The Nook
description: Cozy modern cottage studio retreat in the Adelaide Hills offering a seamless blend of contemporary living and rustic charm amidst nature.

name: Adelaide Meridien Hotel & Apartments
description: Practical comfortable rooms and spacious apartments with outdoor swimming pool and hot tub in North Adelaide, offering great value for city breaks and family stays.

name: Peaceful Eco Retreat
description: Eco-friendly retreat in Echunga in the Adelaide Hills offering a sustainable getaway surrounded by native bushland.

name: The Lane Vineyard Accommodation
description: Accommodation set within the celebrated Lane Vineyard in the Adelaide Hills, offering immersive wine-country experiences.

name: Ramsgate Hotel by Nightcap Social
description: 3-star LGBTQIA-friendly hotel near Henley Beach with stylish accommodations and vibrant communal spaces near Adelaide's nearest beach to the CBD.

name: Glenelg Beachside Apartments
description: 4-star apartments only 150 metres from Glenelg Pier and right near sandy beach, offering self-contained beachside living.

name: COMO The Treasury

name: The Melbourne Hotel

name: Quay Perth

name: European Hotel (formerly Miss Maud Swedish Hotel)

name: Kings Park Motel

name: Hostel G Perth

name: Garde Hotel

name: Hougoumont Hotel
description: Innovative boutique hotel constructed from repurposed shipping containers creating ship cabin-inspired rooms, with complimentary wine and cheese evenings and a prime Bannister Street location.

name: The National Hotel
description: Award-winning restored heritage pub with 12 individually styled luxury ensuite rooms, a spectacular rooftop bar, wrap-around verandas, and live music daily on High Street.

name: The Local Hotel

name: The Federal Boutique Hotel

name: Owston Hotel
description: Intimate 11-room boutique retreat on heritage High Street by the team behind Vin Populi and Common Bakery, blending heritage restoration with locally curated interiors by Rezen Studio.

name: New Haus
description: Modern executive-style hotel in Fremantle's heritage-listed West End, recently refurbished with king beds, new lighting, and smart TVs throughout spacious rooms and suites.

name: Port Mill Bed & Breakfast
description: Luxury B&B in a restored 1862 flour mill housing four well-appointed suites with private balconies and views over Fremantle and the ocean marina, steps from the Cappuccino Strip.

name: Fothergills of Fremantle

name: Fremantle Bed and Breakfast

name: Terrace Central B&B Hotel

name: Fremantle Colonial Cottages

name: Be. Fremantle

name: Tradewinds Hotel

name: Norfolk Hotel

name: Cottesloe Beach Hotel
description: Prendiville Group beachside hotel 200 metres from Cottesloe Beach with three ocean-view restaurants, outdoor areas, and classic coastal Australian pub character.

name: Ocean Beach Hotel

description: Independently run B&B in Mosman Park, a short drive from Cottesloe Beach and Fremantle, with highly rated breakfast service, free WiFi and private parking.

name: Sorrento Beach Bed & Breakfast
description: Boutique B&B only 600 metres from Sorrento Beach offering spacious accommodation with free parking and WiFi, just 20 minutes from Perth city centre.

name: Seashells Scarborough
description: Beachfront apartment resort overlooking Scarborough Beach with ocean-view rooms, private balconies, outdoor pool, and proximity to the revitalised Scarborough foreshore.

name: Durham Lodge Bed & Breakfast
description: Boutique B&B in the heart of Victoria Park offering short and long stay accommodation for professionals and couples, with continental breakfast including fresh fruits and yoghurt.

name: Durack House Bed & Breakfast

name: Palms Bed & Breakfast
description: Four-star B&B near Perth's entertainment district with air-conditioned rooms, outdoor swimming pool, free continental breakfast, and WiFi in public areas.

name: Epsom on Swan Bed & Breakfast
description: Adults-only charming retreat along the Swan River in Ascot run by hosts Ann and Jordan, offering daily continental breakfast and a peaceful riverside setting.

name: Rosebridge House Bed & Breakfast

name: Witch's Hat Backpackers

name: Samphire Rottnest

name: Hotel Rottnest

name: Discovery Rottnest Island

name: The Lodge Wadjemup

name: Quenda Guesthouse

name: Amaroo Retreat & Spa

name: Kalamunda Carriages & Three Gums Cottage

name: Maya Maya at Mandoon Estate

name: Chapel Farm Getaways

name: The Otium at Caversham House

name: Brookleigh Estate
description: Luxury accommodation in a stunning Swan Valley setting offering lakeside and courtyard apartments plus a three-bedroom gatehouse cottage amid beautiful estate grounds.

name: Upper Reach Spa Cottage

name: Lot 113 Vineyard
description: Family-run boutique near the Swan River with two suites featuring kitchenettes, a large shared deck with outdoor kitchen and BBQ, and vineyard views.

name: Cape Lodge

name: Injidup Spa Retreat

name: Losari Retreat

name: Josephine's Luxury Accommodation

name: Yala Yallingup (formerly Empire Retreat)

name: Windmills Break

name: Burnside Organic Farm

name: Gilgara Retreat
description: Adults-only hillside retreat just five minutes from Margaret River town, ideal for couples seeking romantic getaways or digital detox weekends among vineyards and beaches.

name: Littlewood Chalet

name: Forest Rise Chalets & Lodge

name: Wildwood Valley Cottages

name: Bina Maya Yallingup Escape

name: Arimia Restaurant and Winery Guesthouse

name: Aqua Resort

name: Caves House Hotel

name: Farmers' Home Hotel
description: Heritage-listed 1860s boutique hotel in Northam with 16 rooms, modern amenities, the Temperance Bar, and over 150 years of continuous history in the Wheatbelt.

name: York Cottages & Burnley House
description: Self-contained heritage accommodation on 15 acres near York featuring stone cottages with alpaca paddock views and a restored federation-style Burnley House with indoor fires.

name: Premier Mill Hotel
description: Former 1891 flour mill converted to boutique accommodation in Katanning with industrial charm and four unique room categories preserving the building's heritage character.

name: Executive Escapes
description: Accredited Australian tourism company offering curated self-contained beachside homes, villas, and apartments across Cottesloe, Swanbourne, Claremont, and Scarborough.

name: Residence on Langley Park
description: Spacious studio to two-bedroom apartments in East Perth with heated pool, gym, free parking, and Swan River proximity with free bus access to the CBD.

name: Yallingup Glamping Retreat
description: Powered bell tents among lemon and grapefruit trees on 7 acres with onsite spa, yoga instructor, pool, and microbrewery in the Margaret River wine region.

name: Old Picture Theatre
description: Heritage-listed former cinema in Pemberton offering stays in the projection room, dress circle, or stage area near national parks and tall timber forests.

name: Standing Stone House
description: Architectural property in Yallingup with pink walls, exposed brick, art-deco tiles, stone fire pit, and indoor greenery creating a distinctive design retreat.

name: The Lily
description: Extraordinary property featuring a 24.6-metre Dutch Windmill replica and a fully renovated Dakota DC-3 military aircraft converted to sleeping quarters with beds, TV, and dining.

name: Settlers' Cottage at Ampersand Estates
description: Heritage 1870s four-guest cottage within Ampersand Estates featuring on-site winery and stargazing lookout near Pemberton's tall timber country.

name: Island Brook Estate Vineyard and Chalets
description: Vineyard-set self-contained chalets in the Margaret River region offering a peaceful wine country retreat surrounded by native bush and working vines.

name: Aravina Estate
description: Margaret River winery estate offering luxury accommodation alongside cellar door, restaurant, and a unique collection of vintage cars in a stunning rural setting.

name: Citadines St Georges Terrace
description: 85 modern studio and one-bedroom serviced apartments on St Georges Terrace within walking distance of Perth's galleries, restaurants, and entertainment precinct.

name: Heyscape Kings Park
description: Three luxe tiny cabins within Kings Park offering uninterrupted views of the Swan River or Perth city skyline, a unique off-grid urban glamping experience.

name: Nesci Estate Wine Farm Cabin (Heyscape Chittering)
description: Off-grid luxe tiny home on the grounds of Nesci Estates Wine Farm in the Chittering Valley, combining vineyard views with sustainable cabin design.

name: Heyscape Avon Valley
description: Eco-friendly off-grid tiny cabin farm stay in the Toodyay area with premium amenities, pet-friendly accommodation, and stunning Avon Valley landscape.

name: Kangaroo Valley Homestead
description: Romantic couples retreat near Mundaring with side-by-side outdoor baths and a heritage-style homestead set in the peaceful Perth Hills bushland.

name: Boyup Brook Farmstay
description: Tin shed accommodation on a 500-acre property near the Blackwood River offering home-cooked meals, indoor fireplace, and authentic Australian rural life experience.

name: Quay Hotel Perth (by Crown)
description: Perth CBD hotel overlooking Elizabeth Quay with earthy tones and locally sourced materials, rooftop HQ Bar & Kitchen, and co-working spaces.

name: HYDE Perth
description: Lifestyle hotel with 121 naturally-textured rooms, year-round heated outdoor pool with cabanas, and FARRA, a Grecian-inspired restaurant and bar.

name: East Hotel
description: A 140-room boutique hotel with Italian roots in Kingston, featuring moody and sumptuous interiors, apartment-style rooms, and onsite dining including Italian and Asian restaurants.

name: Hotel Kurrajong Canberra
description: Iconic 1926 Art Deco heritage hotel designed by John Smith Murdoch, where Prime Minister Ben Chifley lived during his term in office. Features 147 refurbished rooms with terrace gardens.

name: Hotel Realm
description: Luxury 163-room hotel by independent Doma Hotels group in the Realm Precinct near Parliament House, with spa, wellness facilities, and 24-hour room service.

name: Little National Hotel
description: Compact-luxury concept by Doma Hotels with Japanese-influenced design, four-poster beds, floor-to-ceiling windows, and luxurious communal spaces resembling an exclusive executive lounge.

name: Burbury Hotel & Apartments
description: Mixed hotel and apartment accommodation by Doma Hotels in the Realm Precinct, known as a dog-friendly stay with pet amenities and rooftop views.

name: Brassey Hotel
description: Heritage-listed hotel by Doma Hotels offering classic elegance in the Parliamentary Triangle area, with a warm, traditional atmosphere.

name: Pinnacle Apartments
description: Doma Hotels property offering one to four bedroom fully serviced apartments in a tree-lined Kingston street, close to boutique shopping and cosmopolitan cafes.

name: Deco Hotel
description: Art Deco-inspired boutique hotel by Capital Hotels group, blending vintage charm with modern amenities, set moments from Braddon and the CBD.

name: Avenue Hotel Canberra
description: 213-room property by Capital Hotels with New York-inspired design on Northbourne Avenue, featuring Marble and Grain steakhouse and varied room types.

name: Pavilion on Northbourne
description: Locally owned hotel by Capital Hotels since 1981, featuring tropical-inspired decor with garden courtyards, outdoor pool, and on-site dining in Dickson.

name: Jamala Wildlife Lodge
description: Family-owned five-star lodge within the National Zoo, offering safari-style rooms including uShaka Lodge, Giraffe Treehouses, and Jungle Bungalows with close animal encounters.

name: Canberra Rex Hotel
description: Canberra icon since 1960 combining old-world charm with modern comforts, featuring 121 rooms, the Howling Moon rooftop bar, and a prime Braddon location.

name: Knightsbridge Canberra
description: Sophisticated boutique hotel in the Parliamentary Triangle precinct, 100 metres from Kingston, with complimentary parking and a warm, inviting ambiance.

name: Forrest Hotel and Apartments
description: Family-owned and operated property with 75 motel-style rooms and 40 serviced apartments, nestled near Parliament House and a short stroll from Manuka village.

name: Manuka Park Serviced Apartments
description: One of Canberra's first serviced apartment complexes since 1985, offering fully self-contained apartments closest to the Manuka shopping precinct.

name: Oxley Court Serviced Apartments
description: Secluded apartment accommodation between Manuka and Kingston precincts, featuring large kitchens, extended-stay friendly, with free undercover parking.

name: Green Rooms Braddon
description: Three custom-built luxury themed micro apartments inspired by tiny home design, each with unique themes like 'Jungle', featuring bespoke joinery and treeline views.

name: And Apartments
description: Brand-new boutique complex on Lonsdale Street with oversized windows, king-sized beds, gourmet kitchenettes, and ground-floor B-SIDE restaurant.

name: Accommodate Canberra - New Acton
description: Premium independently managed luxury serviced apartments in the New Acton cultural precinct, with resort-style facilities and weekly housekeeping.

name: Canberra Avenue Villas
description: Self-contained villa accommodation complex with private bathrooms and kitchenettes, ideal for groups, families, couples and long stays.

name: University House ANU
description: Elegant 1950s-designed accommodation on the ANU campus with a tranquil garden featuring beautiful trees and a pond in the central quadrangle.

name: Canberra Carotel Motel
description: Family-friendly independent motel with 23 fully self-contained accommodations featuring two bedrooms, kitchen, and veranda, 10 minutes from the city centre.

name: The Evergreen B&B
description: Centrally located bed and breakfast on a leafy Braddon street with seasonal outdoor pool, library, free parking, and just a 7-minute walk to the city.

name: The Pommy Tree Bed & Breakfast
description: Highly rated B&B in Evatt offering full English breakfast, two en-suite rooms, swimming pool, and easy access to Palmerville heritage park.

name: Georgie's Boutique B&B
description: Boutique bed and breakfast in Monash near nature reserves, featuring daily English breakfast, soundproof rooms, and premium amenities.

name: Canberra Retreat Bed and Breakfast
description: Exclusive-use self-contained B&B in a quiet leafy suburb with private entrance, offering home comforts with local knowledge for short breaks or longer getaways.

name: Ivy and Roses Boutique Apartments
description: Boutique apartments in Franklin featuring free breakfast, self parking, in-room kitchens, balconies, and complimentary WiFi.

name: Gooromon Park Cottages
description: Rural cottages in the Hall wine-growing hills with private patios overlooking the Brindabella Mountains, near Surveyor's Hill Winery.

name: Beltana Farm
description: Farm stay in Pialligo's trufferie with over 800 truffle trees, offering cottages and uniquely styled yurts with an onsite restaurant serving seasonal farmhouse-inspired cuisine.

name: Cubby and Co at Mount Majura Vineyard
description: Handcrafted tiny homes and bell tents nestled in the vines at Mount Majura Vineyard, offering breathtaking views and a secluded glamping experience.

name: Abode Murrumbateman
description: Iconic Hotels property with 50 studio rooms in the heart of cool-climate wine country, featuring a pantry stocked with 40+ local wines and free bike hire.

name: Abode Kingston
description: Iconic Hotels property perfectly located in inner-south Canberra with washer/dryer, kitchenette, TV casting, and ample space in each room.

name: Abode Woden
description: Iconic Hotels property in south Canberra offering 152 stylishly appointed self-contained apartment-style rooms.

name: Abode Belconnen
description: Iconic Hotels property in Canberra's north offering apartment-style accommodation with gourmet pantry and suburban character.

name: Abode Gungahlin
description: The original Abode Hotels property opened in 2010, the first in the Iconic Hotels portfolio, offering apartment-style accommodation in Canberra's north.

name: Tallagandra Hill Winery Cottages
description: Three self-contained vineyard cottages 30 minutes from Canberra, each with queen bed, kitchen, covered deck, complimentary charcuterie, estate wines, and breakfast.

name: Lake George Winery Accommodation
description: Pet-friendly eco-stays within a 300-acre vineyard including tiny houses, Olive Cottage, and Little George, with the Westering onsite restaurant.

name: Surveyors Hill Vineyards B&B
description: Family-owned 100-acre vineyard property near Hall offering accommodation with breakfast and dinner on request, 25 minutes from Canberra, with a free bottle of wine per booking.

name: Wallaroo Wines and Estate
description: Georgian-style homestead accommodation on a vineyard established in 1993, offering private group wine tastings and a venue for getaways and events.

name: Redbrow Garden Guesthouse
description: Nature-based accommodation on the outskirts of Canberra near the wine region, set in an ecologically diverse wetland and forest oasis with four themed en-suite rooms and AAA 4.5-star rating.

name: DeVine 2582
description: Off-grid luxury retreat powered by solar energy at the tip of the Murrumbateman Winery Trail, near acclaimed wineries Clonakilla and Vintner's Daughter.

name: Murrumbateman Country Inn
description: Comfortable 14-room motel with on-site bistro, fireplaces for winter, and shaded verandahs for summer, 10 minutes from Yass and 30 from Canberra.

name: Yarrh Wines Accommodation
description: Two tiny homes (Mawson and Kira) by Cubby and Co set in scenic Yass River Valley bushland with spectacular views across vineyards and farms.

name: The Nest at Gundaroo
description: Award-winning family-owned boutique hotel in a historic rural village with five self-contained villas featuring king beds, wood-fired heaters, and a heritage cottage.

name: Gundaroo Colonial Inn
description: Heritage pub established in 1872, also known as Matt Crowe's Wine Bar, offering accommodation with restaurant, bar, and free WiFi, 30 minutes from Canberra.

name: The Old Stone House
description: Boutique hotel built in the 1860s in a heritage village 40km from Canberra, offering characterful accommodation in one of Australia's 2025 Small Tourism Town winners.

name: The Carrington Inn
description: Heritage hotel operating since 1885 with 26 comfortable suites, quality dining, gardens surrounded by mature European Elm trees in the heart of Bungendore.

name: Butmaroo Station
description: Working 3,000-acre sheep and cattle property offering heritage farm stay accommodation with restored historic buildings in the Bungendore district.

name: The Doncaster
description: Seven meticulously restored rooms amid Georgian streets of heritage-listed Braidwood, with bespoke botanical bedheads, handmade joinery, and soft wooden floorboards.

name: Mona Farm
description: 124-acre luxury destination with multiple historic guest houses including the Gardener's Cottage and Long Barn, set among award-winning gardens near Braidwood.

name: Mona Farm - Shearer's Quarters
description: Luxurious two-person guest house dating to 1873 with original brickwork, Japanese-inspired bath house, heated spa, wood-burning fireplaces, and views of Mona Creek.

name: Bedervale
description: Beautifully preserved 1836 homestead B&B on an expansive 450-hectare working cattle and sheep property near Braidwood, immersing guests in history and nature.

name: Tidmarsh
description: Historic property built in 1856 and modelled on a 19th-century rural Irish inn in heritage-listed Braidwood, sleeping up to 8 guests.

name: Collector Cottage
description: Recently renovated self-contained cottage on 2,000sqm of lush land with 150+ roses and views of the Cullerin Range, including a flower workshop space.

name: Bushranger Hotel
description: Heritage-listed 1860s pub with Victorian-Georgian design on the site of the famous 1865 Ben Hall gang raid, offering 6 rooms with period atmosphere, 40 minutes north of Canberra.

name: Colonial Lodge Motor Inn
description: Family-owned boutique motel in Yass offering updated suites with modern furnishings, friendly hospitality and excellent service.

name: Swaggers Motor Inn
description: Independent motel in Yass with BBQ area, landscaped gardens, patio dining, and bar, 15 minutes' walk from the town centre.

name: Warroo Homestead
description: Elegantly renovated 1930s farmhouse with stunning views over the Murrumbidgee River, featuring original features, roaring fireplace, and 15-metre pool, sleeping up to 8.

name: Hotel Binalong
description: Authentic family-run country pub with traditional rooms, genuine country hospitality, cold beer, and generous meals in the Yass Valley.

name: Pialligo Cottage at Pialligo Estate
description: Four-bedroom fully furnished private cottage in Pialligo with 150sqm of living space, fully equipped kitchen, sunny courtyard, and proximity to farm-gate venues.

name: Canberra Heritage House
description: Charming heritage residence across from the Australian War Memorial with art-filled walls and plush interiors, near Reid Park and Anzac Parade.

name: Snowgums Mountain Retreat
description: Sleek modern cabin sleeping up to eight in a deep valley with views of Mount Clear, two hours south of Canberra through Namadgi National Park.

name: Unyoked Cabins Near Canberra
description: Off-grid tiny house cabins tucked among NSW bushland just outside Canberra, many pet-friendly, designed to feel remote without going far.

name: Hillview Farmstay
description: Working beef farm on nearly 1,000 acres near Gundagai offering 9 self-contained luxury cottages, glamping safari tent, dome tent, pool, and tennis court.

name: Statesman Hotel
description: Independent hotel with bistro and bar less than 10 minutes' drive from Parliament House, offering a transfer service and casual dining.

name: Hamilton's Queanbeyan Motel
description: Officially rated 4.5-star independent motel with contemporary features and boutique styling, minutes from Canberra's tourist precinct.

name: Hotel Queanbeyan
description: Independent hotel in Queanbeyan with restaurant, free parking, and bar, convenient to Canberra attractions.

name: Captains Flat Hotel
description: Restored historic country pub about an hour from Canberra, recently renovated with modern amenities, welcoming weekend visitors and cyclists.

name: Good Hope Resort
description: Natural lake resort on Lake Burrinjuck 15 minutes from Yass, offering cabins and cottages right on the water with pool, tennis court, and swimming.

name: Saddle Camp Tiny House
description: Charming tiny house on a horse farm near Braidwood, one hour from Canberra, where children can feed horses and take riding lessons.

name: Mona Farm - Long Barn
description: Restored luxury accommodation based on the original 1860s building design at Mona Farm, sleeping up to 8 guests in heritage surrounds near Braidwood.

name: Mona Farm - Gardener's Cottage
description: Charming weatherboard cottage in the heart of Braidwood at Mona Farm, sleeping up to 10 guests with access to the 124-acre property and award-winning gardens.

name: Grevillea Cottage
description: Private self-contained 2-bedroom cottage on 1-acre property near Murrumbateman wineries, with ensuite, modern kitchen, deck overlooking paddocks, and breakfast basket.

name: Kimo Estate Ecohuts
description: Off-grid eco-luxurious huts on a stunning rural estate near Gundagai, offering romantic and secluded getaways powered by sustainable energy.

name: Warrambui Retreat
description: Unique retreat and conference centre 40 minutes north of Canberra with full catering and accommodation, set in a natural bush setting.

name: Crafters Cabin
description: Two eco-friendly cabins near Jindabyne with minimalist stone design and wood-fired hot tubs on deck, a 2-hour drive from Canberra in the alpine valley.

name: Abode Malua Bay
description: Iconic Hotels property extending the Abode brand to the NSW South Coast, offering the group's signature apartment-style accommodation by the beach.

name: Argus Hotel Darwin
description: Independently owned boutique hotel in Darwin CBD offering modern contemporary rooms with Juliet balconies, Aboriginal artwork by Merrepen Arts, and views over the city. Close to Smith Street Mall and Darwin Waterfront.

name: Darwin City Hotel
description: Family-owned and operated 4-star boutique hotel on Smith Street with a distinctive frosted glass geometric facade. Offers 91 rooms including self-contained apartments with city views.

name: DCH on Mitchell
description: The newest addition from the family behind Darwin City Hotel, located on Mitchell Street in the heart of Darwin's entertainment precinct. Boutique rooms with walk-in showers and LCD TVs.

name: The Heritage Darwin
description: Recently refurbished independent hotel in Darwin CBD opposite the Entertainment Centre. Heritage King and Twin rooms with plush beds, pool, and garden terrace.

name: H on Mitchell Apartment Hotel
description: 4-star apartment hotel in the heart of Darwin CBD with over 180 rooms from studios to two-bedroom apartments. Features pool, fitness centre, and conference facilities.

name: Palms City Resort
description: Boutique tropical resort in central Darwin with 89 rooms including hotel rooms and private garden bungalows set among lush tropical gardens and an oasis-style pool.

name: Darwin Waterfront Luxury Suites
description: Independent luxury apartment suites at Kitchener Drive with harbour views, Nespresso machines, and contemporary art. TripAdvisor's number one ranked Darwin accommodation since 2015.

name: Mandalay Luxury Stay
description: NT Tourism Brolga Award-winning colonial stone cottage on Darwin's Esplanade, originally built by Lord McAlpine. Three luxury bedrooms, private pool, and sweeping harbour views from wraparound balcony.

name: Darwin City B&B
description: Intimate bed and breakfast in Larrakeyah with swimming pool and garden, a ten-minute walk to Mindil Beach Markets and Cullen Bay. Complimentary breakfast included.

name: Darwin City Bed & Breakfast (formerly Steeles at Larrakeyah)
description: Quiet, centrally located B&B in inner Darwin run by Janette and Roger Steele. Three air-conditioned rooms opening onto a guest verandah overlooking a saltwater pool and tropical gardens.

name: Frangipanni Bed & Breakfast
description: Single-room tropical B&B in Rapid Creek beneath the family home, offering private access with no other guests. Walking distance to shops and beach with home-style breakfast provisions.

name: Elsey on Parap
description: Proudly independent and 100% Australian-owned apartment hotel since 2005. Twenty-eight executive one and two-bedroom apartments opposite Parap Village with pool and secure parking.

name: Casa on Gregory
description: Independent boutique motel in vibrant Parap opposite the shopping centre, featuring a tropical swimming pool, BBQ area, and sea breezes. Close to Fannie Bay and Mindil Beach Markets.

name: Paravista Motel
description: Independent motel near Cullen Bay Marina with outdoor pool surrounded by garden greenery, free in-room WiFi, and air-conditioned rooms. Close to Smith Street Mall.

name: Cullen Bay Resorts
description: Waterfront resort apartments overlooking Darwin Harbour and Cullen Bay Marina. Self-contained one and two-bedroom apartments with kitchen and laundry, walking distance to restaurants and Mindil Beach.

name: Alkira Darwin
description: Four-star complex in Parap with landscaped tropical gardens and saltwater pool. Spacious rooms from standard to spa suites with modern amenities.

name: The Leprechaun Resort
description: Independent resort in Winnellie offering hotel and self-catering accommodation with two outdoor pools, 24-hour fitness centre, communal kitchen, and concierge services.

name: Vitina Studio Motel
description: Business-friendly independent 3-star motel near Darwin airport with free WiFi and free parking. Practical base for both leisure and corporate travellers.

name: Capricornia Motel
description: Pet-friendly independent motel on East Point Road in Fannie Bay with free WiFi and pool. Relaxed atmosphere close to Fannie Bay Gaol Museum and East Point Reserve.

name: 9at Peninsular
description: Short-term accommodation in Larrakeyah offering a great location as a base for holidays or work in Darwin. Ideal inner-city position between CBD and Mindil Beach.

name: Top End Safari Camp
description: Created by National Geographic's Outback Wrangler Matt Wright, this glamping camp on a floodplain north of Darwin features 15 Lotus Belle tents, helicopter flights, airboat rides, and croc encounters.

name: Bamurru Plains
description: Exclusive wild bush luxury safari camp on the Mary River floodplain adjacent to Kakadu. Ten bungalows and two retreats with mesh walls to immerse guests in wildlife. Airboat safaris and guided experiences included.

name: Wildman Wilderness Lodge
description: Premium wetlands safari lodge on the Mary River between Darwin and Kakadu. Twenty-five rooms in Safari Tents or Habitats with infinity pool, fire pit, and fine dining featuring barramundi, crocodile, and bush spices.

name: Finniss River Lodge
description: Family-owned luxury lodge on a 50,000-acre cattle station, 90 minutes from Darwin. Six suites with all meals, beverages, and daily touring included. Third-generation pastoralists as hosts.

name: Point Stuart Wilderness Lodge
description: Independent lodge in the Mary River Wetlands offering ensuite rooms, safari tents, and caravan sites. Licensed saloon bar and bistro plus Mary River cruises and barramundi fishing tours.

name: Mary River Wilderness Retreat
description: Retreat in the heart of the Mary River wetlands, 90 minutes from Darwin. Safari tents, bungalows, and camping sites set among the iconic Top End wildlife and birdlife.

name: Mt Bundy Station
description: Historical rural retreat on the banks of the Adelaide River, just over an hour from Darwin. Choose from Cook's Cottage, Stockman's Quarters, Billabong House, Monsoon Cabin, or Brahman Bungalow.

name: Bark Hut Inn
description: Historic Northern Territory icon on the Arnhem Highway at the western edge of Kakadu. Built in the wild era of buffalo and crocodile hunting, now a hub for Mary River Wetlands exploration.

name: Cobourg Coastal Camp
description: Exclusive eco-glamping on the northernmost tip of Arnhem Land within Garig Gunak Barlu National Park. Eight solar-powered safari tents for a maximum of 18 guests with freshly caught seafood dinners.

name: Seven Spirit Bay Wilderness Lodge
description: Iconic and remote wilderness lodge on the Cobourg Peninsula accessible only by boat or private airstrip. Habitat villas with king-size beds overlooking Coral Bay. All-inclusive with marine adventures and wildlife encounters.

name: Davidson's Arnhemland Safaris
description: Remote safari lodge at sacred Mt Borradaile on an exclusive 700 sq km lease in northwest Arnhem Land. Private cabins with all touring, meals, and Arnhem Land permits included. Over 20 years of operation.

name: Tiwi Island Retreat
description: Beachfront retreat on Bathurst Island, 30-minute flight from Darwin. Village rooms, ocean rooms, and luxury glamping tents with spa. Ocean-to-plate dining, world-class fishing, and Indigenous culture tours.

name: Batchelor Butterfly Farm
description: Unique accommodation on a butterfly and animal farm 90 minutes from Darwin near Litchfield National Park. Freestanding cabins with ensuite, plus a 3-bedroom homestay sleeping up to 12.

name: Litchfield Safari Camp
description: Located on the western edge of Litchfield National Park just 4km from Wangi Falls. Family cabins, studio-ensuite cabins, and luxury glamping tents on fully grassed, shaded sites.

name: Rum Jungle Bungalows
description: Six detached bungalow rooms set on one acre of tropical gardens in Batchelor, 18km from Litchfield National Park entrance. Queen beds, ensuite, and gourmet tropical breakfast by the pool.

name: Litchfield Outback Resort
description: Independent motel and resort in Batchelor at the gateway to Litchfield. Rooms with private balconies overlooking pool and gardens, on-site Lil'Ripper Cafe with chef-prepared meals.

name: Aurora Kakadu Lodge
description: Independent lodge and caravan park in the heart of Kakadu National Park at Jabiru. Self-contained cabins, lodge rooms, and extensive camping sites with poolside bar and bistro.

name: Cicada Lodge
description: 100% Indigenous-owned luxury lodge by the Jawoyn people inside Nitmiluk National Park on the Katherine River. Eighteen elegant rooms with local Aboriginal artwork, award-winning restaurant, and gorge experiences.

name: Nitmiluk Cabins
description: Self-contained chalets positioned below the escarpment entrance to Nitmiluk Gorge, 600m from cruise departures. One and two-bedroom cabins with resort pool and poolside bar.

name: Knotts Crossing Resort
description: Independent resort set among acres of tropical gardens on the road to Nitmiluk Gorge, 2km from Katherine town centre. Village units, cabins, and camping amid lush surroundings.

name: Paraway Motel
description: Independent motel on O'Shea Terrace in central Katherine, a practical and affordable base for exploring Nitmiluk Gorge, Edith Falls, and Cutta Cutta Caves.

name: Djakanimba Pavilions
description: Four elevated pavilion rooms run by Djilpin Arts Aboriginal Corporation in the community of Beswick, 100km from Katherine. Revenue supports the local community. Custom Indigenous cultural tours available.

name: Manbulloo Homestead Caravan Park
description: Independent homestead on the banks of the Katherine River with cabins, motel rooms, and caravan sites. Just 12km from Katherine town centre in a peaceful riverside setting.

name: Pine Creek Railway Resort
description: Eighteen stylishly furnished cabins with a restaurant built in the shape of two trains at a railway station. Lagoon-style pool in historic Pine Creek at the gateway to Kakadu.

name: Lazy Lizard Tavern and Caravan Park
description: Outback pub and accommodation in historic Pine Creek built from mud brick (termite mounds) and local timber. Relaxed Top End atmosphere with restaurant and birdwatching at Kakadu's doorstep.

name: Mataranka Homestead
description: Iconic outback resort next to thermal pools set in lush tropical rainforest maintaining 34 degrees year-round. Cabins, motel rooms, and camping with genuine outback hospitality.

name: Territory Manor Motel
description: Unique rammed-earth motel accommodation in Mataranka, 100km south of Katherine. Twenty-six rooms with outdoor pool, licensed restaurant, and walking distance to Bitter Springs thermal pools.

name: Bullo River Station
description: 400,000-acre working cattle property and luxury lodge in the Victoria River region near the WA border. Twelve guest rooms plus a remote hilltop hut accessible only by helicopter. Conservation at its core.

name: Lorella Springs Wilderness Park
description: One-million-acre privately run station in the Gulf of Carpentaria with accommodation and camping amid dramatic landscapes. Remote wilderness with thermal springs and waterfalls.

name: Longitude 131
description: Sixteen luxury tented pavilions perched on red sand dunes with uninterrupted Uluru views from every bed. Owned by Baillie Lodges, combining sustainable design with gourmet dining and private touring.

name: Curtin Springs Wayside Inn
description: Multi-generational family cattle station and wayside inn one hour from Uluru. Ensuite rooms, budget rooms, and camping offering a genuine Central Australian pastoral experience.

name: Kings Creek Station
description: Working cattle station 36km from Kings Canyon offering luxury glamping in the Dreamtime Escarpment and Drovers Dream tents with king beds and air conditioning, set among majestic desert oaks.

name: Wilmots on Dixon
description: Family-owned B&B in Alice Springs run by hosts Ian and Helen since 1996. Private rooms with garden or pool views, complimentary bikes, outdoor pool, and highly praised buffet breakfast.

name: Squeakywindmill Boutique Tent B&B
description: Luxury glamping tent B&B outside Alice Springs with mountain views, private terrace, fully equipped kitchenette, walk-in shower, and bathrobes. A unique outback experience.

name: Over the Hump
description: Unique self-contained stable accommodation in Alice Springs where guests enjoy visits from local kangaroos, goannas, and dingoes. A truly distinctive outback stay.

name: Desert Palms Alice Springs
description: Eighty individual studio villas set around a free-form pool with waterfall, surrounded by tropical gardens on the banks of the Todd River. Each villa has ensuite, kitchenette, and verandah.

name: Alice on Todd Apartments
description: Fifty-seven self-contained apartments overlooking the Todd River, from studios to two-bedroom deluxe. Independent property with outdoor pool and home-away-from-home feel.

name: Desert Rose Inn
description: Budget-friendly independent motel in the heart of Alice Springs with saltwater pool, communal kitchen, BBQ area, and guest lounge. Practical outback base near Alice Springs Train Station.

name: Elkira Court Motel
description: Independent motel in downtown Alice Springs at the Todd Mall with 58 rooms, outdoor pool, barbecue facilities, and on-site restaurant. Central location near Aboriginal art galleries.

name: Annie's Place
description: Independent guesthouse on Traeger Avenue in The Gap, about 1.2km from Alice Springs centre. Affordable and characterful accommodation in the Red Centre.

name: Heavitree Gap Outback Lodge
description: Independent lodge at the southern entrance to Alice Springs nestled at the base of the MacDonnell Ranges. Hand-feed wild black-footed rock wallabies at sunset. Eighty rooms with balconies and caravan sites.

name: Safari Lodge Motel
description: Award-winning, proudly 100% Aboriginal-owned motel in the heart of Tennant Creek. A genuine outback stay supporting Indigenous enterprise on the Stuart Highway.

name: Bluestone Motor Inn
description: Independent motor inn in Tennant Creek with outdoor pool and restaurant. A comfortable and practical stop on the Stuart Highway between Alice Springs and Darwin.

name: Goldfields Hotel Motel
description: Independent 3-star venue in Tennant Creek with 24 air-conditioned rooms, two pubs, and a dining room. Classic outback hotel on the Stuart Highway.

name: Tilmouth Well Roadhouse
description: Remote outback roadhouse on the iconic Tanami Road, 200km northwest of Alice Springs. Air-conditioned rooms, restaurant and bar, pool, and sprawling lawns under vast outback skies.

name: Batchelor Holiday Park
description: Independent holiday park at the gateway to Litchfield National Park, 98km south of Darwin. Family cabins, luxury two-bedroom villas, and a range of caravan and camping sites.
